nyheter.title: Norfolk Sailor Pleads Guilty to Murder of Fellow Sailor
nyheter.summary: NAVAL STATION NORFOLK, Va. — A Norfolk sailor pleaded guilty Monday to murdering a fellow sailor in May 2025 as part of a plea agreement that will see him sentenced to a minimum of 40 years in a federal prison. Culinary Specialist 3rd Class Jeremiah Copeland appeared before a military judge for a general court-martial and sentencing in a courtroom across the street from the barracks in which he admitted to murdering CS3 Angelina Resendiz. nyheter.summary: China deployed six cutters and patrol ships east of the Luzon Strait in its second “special maritime law enforcement operation” following a diplomatic row with the Philippines and Japan. On Saturday, China’s Ministry of Transport led a mission involving the Fujian and Guangdong regional maritime security administrations east of Taiwan. The INDOPACOM post did not indicate whether the U.S. took possession of MT Davina (IMO: 9259367), which has gone by other names, including MT Lenore. nyheter.title: Leaders of U.S. Navy’s Ship Maintenance Facility in Japan Removed from Command
nyheter.summary: The leadership of the Navy’s primary ship maintenance facility in Japan was removed from command this week, the service announced Wednesday. U.S. Naval Ship Repair Facility and Japan Regional Maintenance Center (SRF-JRMC) commander Capt. Wendel Penetrante, executive officer Capt. Edwin Catubig and Master Chief Petty Officer Thomas Howell were removed from their positions “due to a loss of confidence in their ability to command” by Navy Regional Maintenance Center commander Rear Adm. nyheter.title: Task Force Ashland Returns to California from 4-month Indo-Pacific Deployment
nyheter.summary: Specialized Marine Air-Ground Task Force Ashland recently completed a four-month deployment to the Indo-Pacific, returning to California in the past week. The Marine Corps element of TF Ashland returned Friday to Camp Pendleton, disembarking from amphibious dock landing ship USS Ashland (LSD-48). The ship arrived Monday at its homeport of San Diego, just over four months after it departed Jan. 24. nyheter.title: Philippines Progresses Japanese Destroyer Transfer Talks, Receives American Cutter at Shangri-La
nyheter.summary: The Philippines is set to receive transfers of American and Japanese vessels to bolster its Coast Guard and Navy fleets operating in the South China Sea. American and Japanese defense officials pledged to reinforce Manila’s fleet with recently and soon-to-be decommissioned vessels over the weekend following talks on the sidelines of the International Institute of Strategic Studies Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore held Friday to Sunday. nyheter.title: French Navy Seizes Sanctioned Russian Tanker, 4th Since September
nyheter.summary: The French Navy seized sanctioned tanker MT Tagor on Sunday while it operated in the Atlantic, marking the service’s fourth shadow fleet seizure since September. The tanker was seized on suspicion of flying a false Cameroonian flag and diverted to France, arriving Tuesday in Douarnenez Bay where the ship is detained, the Maritime Prefecture of the Atlantic announced Monday. nyheter.summary: The People’s Liberation Army Navy (PLAN) Liaoning Carrier Strike Group has conducted 170 sorties in the past week as the group trains in the Philippine Sea east of Luzon while shadowed by the Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force. rov_teknologi.summary: Sailors stationed at Naval Base Kitsap, Wash., and Naval Construction Battalion Center Gulfport, Miss., will now have access to a wider variety of food options as part of the Navy’s efforts to improve sailor quality of life. rov_teknologi.title: Specialized Group of 1,300 Marines, Sailors Take Over SOUTHCOM Duties As 22nd MEU Heads Home
rov_teknologi.summary: A specialized group of Marines operating in SOUTHCOM will now support Operation Southern Spear, the U.S. campaign to stop maritime drug trafficking, as the 22nd Marine Expeditionary Unit heads home. The 24th Marine Expeditionary Unit will operate as Littoral Combat Force-24, providing a Marine Air-Ground Task Force that will work under the Joint Task Force Southern Spear, the Marine Corps announced Friday. rov_teknologi.summary: The Royal Navy’s mine countermeasures mothership RFA Lyme Bay (L3007) departed Tuesday from Gibraltar as the U.K. and France prepare to launch a multinational military mission to ensure freedom of navigation through the strait.
